watchdog_orgs_searched: Democracy for the Arab World Now (DAWN)
adversarial_framing_check_notes: Lead Regional stories (US-Saudi civil nuclear deal; Houthi tanker strikes on Saudi vessels in the Red Sea) name Saudi Arabia in a geopolitical/security context. Checked Al Jazeera (largely factual/neutral on both events), Middle East Eye (Qatar-linked), and Iranian state media (Press TV). Two adversarially-framed in-window items confirmed and included in Negative Articles: Middle East Eye (Saudi framed as reckless proliferation risk) and Press TV (Saudi framed as aggressor/besieger under 'siege for a siege').
negative_hits_detail: Middle East Eye - nuclear proliferation-risk framing (adversarial-framing check)
Press TV (Iranian state media) - 'siege for a siege' aggressor framing (adversarial-framing check)
AGBI - Sindalah/NEOM giga-project delivery-scrutiny feature (themed: Vision 2030 delivery / investment scrutiny), surfaced via the General/Macro Press outlet sweep
notes: All 13 negative themes and all 9 watchdog/NGO organizations were searched individually; none produced a standalone in-window (2026-07-22 06:00 UTC to 2026-07-23) hit from an eligible outlet. The three Negative Articles items all derive from the day's two lead Saudi stories (two via the adversarial-framing check) plus one giga-project delivery-scrutiny feature surfaced via the outlet sweep. Israeli outlets (e.g. Haaretz coverage of the nuclear deal's Israeli-concern angle) and Saudi-owned outlets were excluded per the hard source-eligibility rules.
